title: quercus reticulata f. applanata
intro: Quercus reticulata f. applanata, also known as Japanese dwarf oak, is a variant of the Quercus reticulata tree, characterized by its compact growth habit and broad, flat leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Quercus reticulata f. applanata are attributed to its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a potential remedy for various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Quercus reticulata f. applanata is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine and Japanese kampo treatments.
